questions.question: Which of these is considered a 'Vanity Metric' that provides little direct insight into business health?

options.text: Total Number of Website Page Views

questions.explanation: Page views tell you people are arriving, but without conversion context, they don't predict revenue or business growth.

questions.question: How do you calculate Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)?

options.text: (Total Sales & Marketing Spend) / (Number of New Customers Acquired)

questions.explanation: CAC is the average amount of money spent to acquire a single customer during a specific period.

questions.question: What is the primary significance of a high 'Lead Velocity Rate' (LVR)?

options.text: It means your website is loading faster than average.
It is a leading indicator that revenue will likely increase in the coming months.
It means you are spending too much on advertising.
It indicates that customers are unsubscribing from your emails.

questions.explanation: LVR tracks the growth of qualified leads. Since leads precede sales, a growing LVR predicts future revenue growth.

questions.question: According to the lesson, what is the 'target' LTV:CAC ratio for a healthy, scalable business?
questions.explanation: An LTV:CAC of 3:1 means you make $3 back for every $1 you spend on acquisition, which is the standard benchmark for profitability.

questions.question: Which dashboard 'Level' is recommended for founders with fewer than 100 leads per month?

options.text: Level 1: The Simple & Manual Google Sheet
Level 3: The Automated Looker Studio Engine

questions.explanation: At lower volumes, the act of manually entering data into a sheet forces the founder to engage with and understand their numbers.

questions.question: Identify your 'North Star' metric—the one number that matters most in your business right now. Why did you choose it? Calculate your current estimated LTV and CAC. Is your LTV:CAC ratio above or below the 3:1 target? What is one specific action you can take to improve it?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the student's North Star choice and LTV:CAC calculation. Check if they have a realistic plan to improve the ratio (either by lowering CAC or increasing LTV). Provide a tactical suggestion for their specific business type (SaaS vs Coach).
